title: NOC 62024 – Cleaning Supervisors | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 62024 (Cleaning Supervisors) play a critical role in maintaining Canada’s public and private infrastructure. From commercial offices to high-security labs and residential complexes, these TEER 2 professionals ensure that health and safety standards are met at scale. For immigration, this role is a "hidden gem," offering high job security and significant opportunities in provinces with large-scale industrial and commercial growth.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level2Major Group62 – Retail sales and service supervisorsSub-Major Group620 – Retail sales and service supervisorsMinor Group6202 – Service supervisorsNOC Code62024

duties: Supervise and coordinate the work of light-duty, heavy-duty, and specialized cleaners.
Inspect sites or facilities to ensure cleaning standards are met.
Hire and train cleaning staff in procedures and safety protocols.
Prepare work schedules and estimate cleaning costs.
Recommend or arrange for additional services such as painting or structural repairs.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Previous experience as a light or heavy-duty cleaner is mandatory.
Knowledge of chemical safety (WHMIS) is essential.
job_outlook: The outlook is Stable to Good. Over 23,000 job openings are projected as Canada builds more commercial and high-density residential spaces.
labour_market_demand: Post-pandemic, the demand for specialized cleaning supervision has skyrocketed. Commercial real estate in major cities and the industrial sector in the Prairies are in constant need of supervisors who understand chemical safety and industrial hygiene.
Industrial Exposure: Opportunities to work in specialized sectors like aviation and medicine.
Managerial Opportunity: Quick progression to regional operations manager roles.
Job Stability: Cleaning is a recession-proof essential service in Canada.
pathways: Canadian Experience Class (CEC): Ideal for those with 12 months of local work.
Manitoba Skilled Worker in Manitoba Stream: A strong regional pathway.
Ontario Employer Job Offer: For those with job offers in the commercial service sector.
